display.purpose: Article titles and glossary term names. The only place the system goes large.

headline.purpose: Article-card titles in home and category lists.

body.purpose: Article body and most prose. Capped at 65–75ch.

label.purpose: Glossary section titles. Uppercase, letter-spaced. Reserved for structural markers, never in-prose emphasis.

kicker.purpose: Article-details strip (date, reading time, tags) above the article title.

shadows.name: l1
shadows.purpose: Default resting state for cards, sidebar widgets, series-nav. Almost imperceptible — separation without drama.

shadows.name: l2
shadows.purpose: Article-card hover. Paired with translateY(-2px) and 0.3s ease.

shadows.name: l3
shadows.purpose: Heavier elevation; reserved for modal-class surfaces.

shadows.name: l4
shadows.purpose: Top of stack; reserve for true overlays.

shadows.name: popover
shadows.purpose: Glossary popover floating over running text.

shadows.name: popover-dark
shadows.purpose: Glossary popover, dark mode (heavier so it detaches against dark surface).

shadows.name: projects-card-hover
shadows.purpose: Projects banner card on hover; heavier because baseline elevation is taller.

motion.name: ease-default
motion.purpose: Standard easing for card hover (box-shadow 0.3s, transform 0.2s).

motion.name: ease-fast
motion.purpose: Snappier interactions: category links, glossary popover, pagination (0.15–0.2s).

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.purpose: All animation/transition durations when prefers-reduced-motion is set. Reading-progress bar transitions to 'none'.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.value: tablet — article font-size shifts 1.6rem → 1.7rem, card padding 20px → 25px

breakpoints.name: xl
breakpoints.value: wide — card padding 25px → 30px, article-card title 2.2rem → 2.4rem, main top padding 35px → 50px

components.name: Article Card
components.description: Canonical post card used on home, category, and series listings. Flat at rest, lifts on hover.
components.html:
May 12, 2026 · 14 min read

Why the next stablecoin cycle will not look like the last one

A read of the regulatory pressure, the protocol-design responses, and the capital flows that are quietly reshaping the category.

Analysis
components.css: .ds-article-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; background: #ffffff; border-radius: 10px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0px 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0px 0px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.06), 0px 0px 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); transition: box-shadow 0.3s ease, transform 0.2s ease; max-width: 480px; } .ds-article-card:hover { box-shadow: 0px 10px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0px 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0px 0px 1px rgba(0,0,0,0.04); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-article-card__cover { display: block; aspect-ratio: 16/9; overflow: hidden; } .ds-article-card__cover img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; display: block; } .ds-article-card__body { padding: 25px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; } .ds-article-card__kicker { font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.05em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #767676; display: flex; gap: 8px; } .ds-article-card__title { font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1.25; letter-spacing: -0.005em; font-feature-settings: 'kern' 1, 'liga' 1; margin: 0; } .ds-article-card__title a { color: #000000; text-decoration: none; } .ds-article-card__title a:hover { color: #34495e; } .ds-article-card__excerpt { font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.55; color: #595959; margin: 0; }

components.name: Category Badge
components.description: Pill badge using one of the five sanctioned category colors with white text. The active variant uses the color-mix tint on glossary term pages.
components.html: Analysis Opinion Technical Lending Experimental
components.css: .ds-category-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 8.8px 17.6px; border-radius: 999px; font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #ffffff; transition: background-color 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; } .ds-category-badge--sage { background-color: #8ea885; } .ds-category-badge--mauve { background-color: #df7988; } .ds-category-badge--ocean { background-color: #0177b8; } .ds-category-badge--ochre { background-color: #806504; } .ds-category-badge--violet { background-color: #6b69d6; } .ds-category-badge:hover { filter: brightness(0.92); }

components.name: Glossary Term Inline Marker
components.description: Signature interactive primitive: a button reset to read as a link with dotted underline, indicating a glossary definition is available on hover.
components.html: Most yield strategies in DeFi rely on some flavor of management — either by hedging it, accepting it, or routing around it via primitives.
components.css: .ds-glossary-term { appearance: none; background: transparent; border: 0; padding: 0; margin: 0; font: inherit; color: #34495e; border-bottom: 1px dotted #34495e; cursor: help; transition: color 0.15s ease, border-bottom-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-glossary-term:hover { color: #2c3e50; border-bottom-color: #2c3e50; } .ds-glossary-term:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #34495e; outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Glossary Popover
components.description: Floats over running text when the inline marker is hovered/focused. Heavier dedicated shadow so it detaches from the prose.
components.html:
Impermanent Loss

The loss an AMM liquidity provider incurs relative to simply holding the two assets, caused by price divergence between the pair.
components.css: .ds-glossary-popover { display: inline-block; max-width: 320px; padding: 13.6px 16px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(218,218,218,0.5); border-radius: 6px; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.18); font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, sans-serif; } .ds-glossary-popover__term { font-size: 15.2px; font-weight: 700; color: #34495e; margin-bottom: 6px; } .ds-glossary-popover__def { font-size: 15.2px; font-weight: 400; line-height: 1.55; color: #000000; margin: 0; }

components.name: Series Navigation
components.description: Inline series-position rail. Uses the only sanctioned colored side-stripe in the system (4px Dissenting Slate left border) because it encodes information, not decoration.
components.html:
In this series
  1. 1The original Curve Wars, revisited
  2. 2How the bribery market reshaped governance
  3. 3What comes after the gauge
components.css: .ds-series-nav { padding: 20px 24px; background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid rgba(218,218,218,0.5); border-left: 4px solid #34495e; border-radius: 10px; font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, sans-serif; max-width: 520px; } .ds-series-nav__title { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; color: #34495e; margin-bottom: 12px; } .ds-series-nav__list { list-style: none; margin: 0; padding: 0; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8.8px; } .ds-series-nav__item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; font-size: 16px; line-height: 1.5; color: #595959; } .ds-series-nav__item a { color: #595959; text-decoration: none; transition: color 0.15s ease; } .ds-series-nav__item a:hover { color: #34495e; } .ds-series-nav__item--current { font-weight: 700; color: #34495e; } .ds-series-nav__badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 26.4px; height: 26.4px; padding: 0 4px; border-radius: 50%; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.06); font-size: 12.8px; font-weight: 700; color: #000000; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-series-nav__badge--current { background: #34495e; color: #ffffff; }

components.name: Projects Banner Card
components.description: Signature: the deep-navy band that breaks the calm baseline. The single most chromatically committed surface; the projects collection earns it.
components.html:
Projects we cover
CR
Curve
Stable-asset AMM, vote-escrow economics
YN
Yearn
Yield aggregator, vault primitives
components.css: .ds-projects-band { padding: 22px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid rgba(247,241,223,0.08); background: #0a1628 linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(12,44,83,0.78), rgba(20,42,78,0.96) 48%, rgba(10,27,54,0.92)); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(247,241,223,0.06), 0 18px 42px rgba(0,0,0,0.18); font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, sans-serif; max-width: 720px; } .ds-projects-band__intro { font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.12em; color: rgba(222,232,249,0.72); margin-bottom: 14px; } .ds-projects-band__grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 14px; } .ds-projects-card { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 14px; min-height: 118px; padding: 16px; border-radius: 6px; background: rgba(4,13,27,0.62); border: 1px solid rgba(247,241,223,0.13); box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(247,241,223,0.06); color: #f7f1df; text-decoration: none; transition: background 0.18s ease, transform 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-projects-card:hover { background: rgba(8,22,43,0.82); transform: translateY(-3px); border-color: rgba(247,241,223,0.22); box-shadow: 0 12px 26px rgba(0,0,0,0.30); } .ds-projects-card__emblem { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 64px; height: 64px; border-radius: 50%; background: radial-gradient(circle, rgba(247,241,223,0.12), rgba(247,241,223,0.02) 62%, transparent 63%); filter: drop-shadow(0 8px 14px rgba(0,0,0,0.28)); font-family: Fraunces, Georgia, serif; font-size: 22px; font-weight: 700; color: #f7f1df; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-projects-card__body { flex: 1; min-width: 0; } .ds-projects-card__name { font-size: 19px; font-weight: 700; color: #f7f1df; line-height: 1.2; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-projects-card__tag { font-size: 13px; color: rgba(222,232,249,0.72); line-height: 1.4; } .ds-projects-card__arrow { color: #f7f1df; opacity: 0.46; flex-shrink: 0; transition: opacity 0.18s ease, transform 0.18s ease; } .ds-projects-card:hover .ds-projects-card__arrow { opacity: 1; transform: scale(1.1); }

components.name: Difficulty Filter Pill
components.description: Filter pill paired with weight increase on activation (color + weight, so the affordance survives color blindness).
components.css: .ds-difficulty-pill { appearance: none; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 2rem; background: transparent; border: 1.5px solid rgba(218,218,218,0.8); font-family: Inter Tight, Lato, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 500; color: #595959; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 0.15s ease, background-color 0.15s ease, color 0.15s ease; } .ds-difficulty-pill:hover { border-color: #34495e; color: #34495e; } .ds-difficulty-pill:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #34495e; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-difficulty-pill--active { font-weight: 700; } .ds-difficulty-pill--beginner.ds-difficulty-pill--active { border-color: #22c55e; background-color: rgba(34,197,94,0.08); color: #15803d; } .ds-difficulty-pill--intermediate.ds-difficulty-pill--active { border-color: #fbbf24; background-color: rgba(251,191,36,0.08); color: #92400e; } .ds-difficulty-pill--expert.ds-difficulty-pill--active { border-color: #f87171; background-color: rgba(248,113,113,0.08); color: #991b1b; }

narrative.overview:
This is the visual register of a long-form DeFi blog whose infrastructure is part of its argument. The site runs on a privacy-respecting, open-source stack (Hugo, self-hosted Matomo with IP anonymisation, IPFS replication, no Google Fonts) and the design treats that sovereignty as a first principle, not a backdrop. There is no third-party font, no embedded tracker, no analytics-funded gradient. What you see is what was shipped, and what was shipped is what the author owns end-to-end.

The aesthetic is punk-archival: an irreverent editorial voice held inside an unfashionable, durable shell. Fraunces carries the headlines with the gravity of a printed quarterly; Lato carries the body with the restraint of a working notebook. A single accent — Dissenting Slate (#34495e) — does almost all the chromatic work, ceding the screen to the writing. Where personality erupts, it is local and earned: the projects band's deep-hull navy, the rotating category palette, the difficulty filters' green/amber/red. Nothing animates that does not need to. Nothing rounds that does not need to. Nothing decorates that does not argue.

The system explicitly refuses four registers (carried verbatim from PRODUCT.md): corporate-fintech polish, SaaS landing templates, Substack-default blog chrome, and hype-template crypto.
keyCharacteristics: One accent doing 90% of the chromatic work, with five category colors and three difficulty colors as the only sanctioned exceptions.
Editorial serif (Fraunces) for display + headlines; humanist sans (Inter Tight) for body. Lato preserved on disk as legacy fallback only.
Flat at rest, lifts on touch — surfaces sit on the page until the reader engages.
10px primary radius, 6px / 8px secondary, full-pill (999px) for badges and related-term chips only.
Both light and dark themes must read in their respective ambient conditions; dark is not a fashion choice.
Sovereign stack: every asset (fonts, scripts, images, analytics) self-hosted or absent.

rules.name: The Sovereign Stack Rule
rules.body: Every color is defined locally. No color, font, or asset may be sourced from a third-party CDN or SaaS dependency that contradicts the editorial position (privacy, openness, sovereignty). If a design choice requires importing Google Fonts, Cloudflare Insights, a Tailwind play-CDN, or any tracker-bearing surface, the choice is wrong before it is drawn.

rules.name: The Earned Accent Rule
rules.body: Dissenting Slate is the only chromatic vote on most pages. The five category colors are sanctioned only on category surfaces. The three difficulty colors are sanctioned only on difficulty pills. Decorative use of any tertiary color is forbidden. Color is signal, never garnish.

rules.name: The No-Pure-Black Rule
rules.body: Light surfaces use #f5f5fa (Paper Bone), never #ffffff for the body. Dark surfaces use #303030 (Slate Night), never #000000. Pure black appears only in card title text on light mode.

rules.name: The One-Serif Rule
rules.body: Fraunces is the only serif. Any future serif need must use Fraunces at a different weight or size; introducing a second serif breaks the editorial signature.

rules.name: The 65-Character Measure Rule
rules.body: Body text never exceeds 75 characters per line. The article container caps it; the glossary term layout caps it harder at 760px. If a future component spans a wider grid, the body text inside it does not.

rules.name: The Uppercase-Means-Structure Rule
rules.body: Uppercase letter-spaced type is reserved for structural labels (glossary section titles, kicker, series-nav title). Never use it for in-prose emphasis.

rules.name: The Flat-at-Rest Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat by default; shadows appear only as a response to interaction (hover, focus, popover open) or to detach from a heavily contrasted surface. A card with a permanent heavy shadow is wrong.

rules.name: The Dark-Mode-Heavier-Shadow Rule
rules.body: Dark-mode shadows are tuned with 3–4× the opacity of light-mode shadows because dark-on-dark elevation only reads when the shadow is dark enough to register. Do not copy light-mode shadow opacities into dark-mode tokens.

rules.name: The Calm-Baseline Rule
rules.body: Most components are quiet (10px radius, Card White, single hairline, no shadow at rest). Sharp moments — the projects band, the active difficulty pill, the category badge, the drop-cap — earn their amplitude by being rare. Doubling the loud surfaces halves their force.

rules.name: The Series-Nav Stripe Exception
rules.body: Side-stripe borders thicker than 1px are banned globally, with one exception: the 4px Dissenting Slate left border on series-nav, because it functions as a structural rail tying sequential posts to a shared identity. Any future use must clear the same bar: encode information, not decorate.

dos: Do use Dissenting Slate (#34495e) as the sole chromatic accent on 90%+ of surfaces. The rarity is the point.
Do use Fraunces 700 with letter-spacing: -0.01em and font-feature-settings: 'kern' 1, 'liga' 1 for every display and headline.
Do keep body text at 1.7rem Lato, line-height 1.85, color Ink Body (#595959) — the AAA-on-Paper-Bone target. Do not lower the contrast back to the theme default.
Do cap body measure at 65–75ch. The glossary template caps at 760px; honor it on every prose-bearing component.
Do keep surfaces flat at rest and let --shadow-l1 → --shadow-l2 + translateY(-2px) carry hover. The system reads as paper-at-rest, paper-in-hand.
Do pair the projects band's chromatic commitment with at least one full hover state — it is the one place the design earns the right to feel rich.
Do carry every category color with white text and every difficulty hue with a weight change, so the affordance survives color blindness.
Do maintain dark-light parity. Both themes are first-class; ship neither without testing the other.
donts: Don't import Google Fonts, Cloudflare Insights, a CDN-hosted analytics snippet, or any third-party asset that contradicts the sovereign-stack posture. Privacy is part of the argument; the stack is part of the design.
Don't drift toward corporate fintech (PRODUCT.md anti-ref): no navy-and-gold trust palette, no stock-photo team grids, no 'trusted by' logo wall.
Don't draft SaaS landing templates (PRODUCT.md anti-ref): no hero-metric + small-label headline, no three identical icon+heading+text cards, no gradient-text H1.
Don't ship Substack-default chrome (PRODUCT.md anti-ref): plain serif on plain white with no identity is forfeit, not safety. This blog has a voice; the design must too.

Don't slip into hype-template crypto (PRODUCT.md anti-ref): no animated mesh gradients, no holographic gloss, no neon-purple-on-pitch-black, no glow buttons, no 'future of finance' hero copy. Punk-archival yes, hype-bro no.

Don't use #000 or #fff for body backgrounds or display type. Paper Bone (#f5f5fa) and Slate Night (#303030) are the canonical surfaces; Card White (#ffffff) is the one sanctioned pure-white, on card interiors only.
Don't add a colored side-stripe (border-left / border-right > 1px in a category hue) anywhere except series-nav. The pattern is banned globally and survives only as the documented exception.
Don't apply background-clip: text over a gradient to any heading. Solid color or nothing.
Don't use category colors decoratively. A teal underline because the section 'feels analytical' violates the Earned Accent Rule. Color is signal.
Don't introduce a second serif. Fraunces is the only serif; any new editorial flourish uses Fraunces at a new weight or size, never a new family.
Don't add em dashes or -- in UX copy (carried from PRODUCT.md). Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods.
Don't add prefers-reduced-motion-violating transforms or animations. The system already respects it; new motion must too.
Don't ship a hover state that animates a CSS layout property (width, padding, margin). Use transform and opacity exclusively.
Don't wrap every component in a card. Series-nav, glossary popover, and article-cards are cards; running prose, the kicker, the difficulty pills, and the breadcrumb are not.
